title: podman compose suggests using ---replace but doesn't have such an option
body:
### Issue Description

If you stop a stack started with `podman compose up` from a compose file, but don't destroy it, when you try to start it again podman will rightly complain that the container names are already taken.
And will say :
> Error: creating container storage: the container name "shiny-container" is already in use by 015180d2e9f53abd16bfe5386961a0c90fc99c6b354eca62a21480e8e9e3e17c. You have to remove that container to be able to reuse that name: that name is already in use, or use --replace to instruct Podman to do so.

But `--replace` is not a compose flag, currently it only exists for  podman create, pod create, run (according to [replace.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/options/replace.md)).

However. compose knows `--force`.

So either compose should respond to `--replace` or it should suggest using `--force` instead 🙂 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.start a stack with `podman compose up` from a compose file
2. ctrl+C
3. start the stack again with `podman compose up`
4. fails as container name is already in use, suggests using `--replace`
5. `podman compose --replace` : exit status 2

### Describe the results you received

"use --replace to instruct Podman" to remove the existing container

### Describe the results you expected

`--replace` is recognised by `podman compose`

Or, at least, the output should suggest: "use --force to instruct Podman" to remove the existing container

comments.body:
podman compose is just a wrapper around calling `docker-compose` or `podman-compose`. In you case you are using podman-compose and this is just calling podman run ... so it has no idea on how it was called and such this is impossible to fix.

You would need to create an issue on the [podman-compose](https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/) repo (which is not maintained by the podman maintainers) and ask them to implement such a flag

I think we could still massage the error message a bit.  Clients going through the REST API would need to be instructed how to replace a container.  Mentioning the --replace flag in those cases can cause confusion.  But I don't have a good proposal how to rephrase the error message to account for both use cases: Podman CLI and Podman REST API.  Maybe after another coffee :^)

>  Clients going through the REST API would need to be instructed how to replace a container. Mentioning the --replace flag in those cases can cause confusion

But we don't! We only add this message in the cli case and podman-compose just runs cli commands.
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2f6fca6edc5c0d4e0a1d758a14c9de7014cd0561/cmd/podman/root.go#L667-L668

There is no replace option in the API, clients already know they have to delete and create a again.

In the case of `podman compose` the line is blurry I guess as it's a CLI command to essentially guide a client talk to the API.

Couldn't we make this error message conditional and only add the --replace note when the command is not compose?

> Couldn't we make this error message conditional and only add the --replace note when the command is not compose?

Why? It is up to podman-compose to create a proper error. 

In our case podman run is called. It cannot have any way of knowing how it was called unless you like to add stupid code the checks parent processes.

Thanks for elaborating, @Luap99!

I was under the impression this would also happen with docker-compose.  But since (as you explained) podman-compose uses podman-run, there's nothing podman could do.

Yeah, `podman compose` is a wrapper around `podman run`, so issue doesn't relate to `podman`.
But still would like to answer that you may be probably looking for `--force-recreate` flag.

As per the docs, it recreate containers even if their configuration and image haven't changed.
